title: Remote work is over. Here's what killed it
post:
Remote work is over. Not because companies forced people back — because AI did.

Think about it. The main case for remote work was talent access and work-life balance. Both are still valid. But AI has made collaboration so much faster that being in the same room matters less.

When you can hop into a Claude-powered war room, share context instantly, and have AI track every decision — physical presence becomes less critical.

The companies bringing people back aren't doing it for culture. They're doing it because they haven't figured out how to do distributed work with AI.

The real lesson: remote work failed not because humans need office presence, but because digital collaboration tools weren't good enough. Now they are.

So the question isn't remote vs. office. It's: are you using AI to collaborate, or are you using Zoom and Slack?

What's your office situation? Thoughts?
